metabolic.subtitle: Energy synthesis & storage
metabolic.why_it_matters: Metabolic dysfunction is a primary driver of chronic disease. Optimizing glucose and insulin markers directly impacts healthspan by addressing the root cause of the Four Horsemen diseases.

why_citations.title: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes — 2024
why_citations.claim_supported: HbA1c ≥ 6.5% as a diagnostic threshold for type 2 diabetes; < 5.7% as normal.

why_citations.title: Outlive — optimal metabolic targets (expert opinion)
why_citations.claim_supported: An optimal HbA1c target below 5.5% for longevity (expert opinion · discuss with clinician).

cardiovascular.subtitle: Lipids & atherosclerotic risk
cardiovascular.why_it_matters: Atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of death worldwide. ApoB / LDL particle burden is the central, causal, and modifiable driver — the lower and earlier, the better.

why_citations.title: ACC/AHA Guideline on the Management of Blood Cholesterol
why_citations.claim_supported: LDL-C and ApoB targets for ASCVD risk reduction; Lp(a) one-time testing.

liver.subtitle: Hepatic function & fibrosis
liver.why_it_matters: The liver is central to metabolism and detoxification. Transaminases (ALT/AST) plus the FIB-4 fibrosis estimate flag fatty-liver and fibrosis risk well before symptoms appear.

kidney.subtitle: Filtration & electrolytes
kidney.why_it_matters: Kidney filtration (eGFR) declines silently with age and metabolic disease. Creatinine, urea and electrolytes track filtration and fluid balance — early detection preserves long-term function.

why_citations.title: New Creatinine- and Cystatin C–Based Equations to Estimate GFR without Race
why_citations.claim_supported: Race-free CKD-EPI 2021 eGFR equation, endorsed by KDIGO.

inflammation_immune.subtitle: Systemic inflammatory load
inflammation_immune.why_it_matters: Chronic low-grade inflammation ("inflammaging") accelerates every Horseman disease. hs-CRP and ESR are the most accessible systemic inflammation markers.

hematologic.subtitle: Red & white cell health
hematologic.why_it_matters: The complete blood count is a window into oxygen-carrying capacity, immune status and marrow health. Hemoglobin, RDW and white-cell lines flag anaemia, inflammation and more.

endocrine_thyroid.subtitle: Thyroid axis
endocrine_thyroid.why_it_matters: The thyroid axis sets metabolic rate. TSH with T3/T4 detects hypo- and hyper-thyroidism, which influence weight, energy, mood and cardiovascular risk.

micronutrient_bone.subtitle: Vitamins, minerals & bone
micronutrient_bone.why_it_matters: Micronutrient status underpins immunity, bone strength and energy. Vitamin D, B12, ferritin and calcium are common, correctable deficiencies with outsized downstream effects.

why_citations.title: Evaluation, Treatment, and Prevention of Vitamin D Deficiency

points.lab: Lifespan Wellness Pvt Ltd - Indira Nagar

hba1c.about: HbA1c reflects your average blood sugar over the past 2–3 months. Values at or above 6.5% are the diagnostic threshold for diabetes in most guidelines.

natural_freq.comparator_desc: About 41 of 100 adults aged 65+ in reference data have HbA1c in the diabetic range.
natural_freq.caveat: Based on US NHANES reference data. May not be calibrated for Indian populations.

citations.title: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes — 2024
citations.claim_supported: HbA1c ≥ 6.5% as a diagnostic threshold for type 2 diabetes; < 5.7% as normal.

citations.title: ICMR Guidelines for Management of Type 2 Diabetes — 2023
citations.claim_supported: Clinical reference range for HbA1c of 4.0–5.6% in non-diabetic adults.

citations.title: Outlive — optimal metabolic targets (expert opinion)
citations.claim_supported: An optimal HbA1c target below 5.5% for longevity (expert opinion · discuss with clinician).

hemoglobin.about: Hemoglobin is the oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells. Low values indicate anaemia; the trend matters more than a single reading.

ldl_cholesterol.about: LDL carries cholesterol into the artery wall. Lower LDL particle burden over a lifetime means less atherosclerosis — for most adults a target below 100 mg/dL is reasonable, lower if at risk.

points.lab: Lifespan Wellness Pvt Ltd - Indira Nagar

citations.title: ACC/AHA Guideline on the Management of Blood Cholesterol
citations.claim_supported: LDL-C and ApoB targets for ASCVD risk reduction; Lp(a) one-time testing.

points.lab: Lifespan Wellness Pvt Ltd - Indira Nagar

tsh.about: TSH is the pituitary signal that drives the thyroid. A high TSH suggests an under-active thyroid; a low TSH an over-active one.

points.lab: Lifespan Wellness Pvt Ltd - Indira Nagar
Lifespan Wellness Pvt Ltd - Indira Nagar
Lifespan Wellness Pvt Ltd - Indira Nagar

sgpt.about: ALT is an enzyme that leaks from liver cells when they are stressed or damaged. Persistently raised ALT is the most common signal of fatty liver disease.

crp.about: C-reactive protein rises with inflammation anywhere in the body. The high-sensitivity assay (hs-CRP) is used to gauge low-grade vascular inflammation.

egfr.about: eGFR estimates how well your kidneys filter blood, from your creatinine, age and sex. Above 90 is normal; a sustained decline below 60 defines chronic kidney disease.

citations.title: New Creatinine- and Cystatin C–Based Equations to Estimate GFR without Race
citations.claim_supported: Race-free CKD-EPI 2021 eGFR equation, endorsed by KDIGO.

caveats: US NHANES reference data, may not be calibrated for Indian populations.

source.title: An epigenetic biomarker of aging for lifespan and healthspan
source.claim_supported: PhenoAge derived from 9 clinical markers + chronological age (NHANES III).

ingestion_jobs.filename: mfine report 100319 Full Body Check Up

checkin.intro: Please indicate for each statement which is closest to how you have been feeling over the past two weeks.

items.prompt: I have felt cheerful and in good spirits.
I have felt calm and relaxed.
I have felt active and vigorous.
I woke up feeling fresh and rested.
My daily life has been filled with things that interest me.

raise_first.body: Your LDL cholesterol is 196 mg/dL — well above the < 100 mg/dL target for cardiovascular risk reduction. Total cholesterol is also elevated. This atherogenic burden is your most important trend to discuss and act on.

citations.title: ACC/AHA Guideline on the Management of Blood Cholesterol
citations.claim_supported: LDL-C and ApoB targets for ASCVD risk reduction; Lp(a) one-time testing.

questions: My Glucose Random is elevated (163 mg/dL, ref 70–140). What is driving this and what should we do?
My HbA1c is elevated (7 %, ref 4.5–6). What is driving this and what should we do?
My Lymphocytes % is elevated (54.7 %, ref 20–45). What is driving this and what should we do?
My Neutrophils % is low (29.8 %, ref 40–75). What is driving this and what should we do?
My MCV is low (81.7 fL, ref 78–98). What is driving this and what should we do?
My Cholesterol Total is elevated (297.2 mg/dL, ref 125–200). What is driving this and what should we do?
My HDL Cholesterol is elevated (81.3 mg/dL, ref 30–65). What is driving this and what should we do?
My LDL Cholesterol is elevated (196 mg/dL, ref 85–130). What is driving this and what should we do?

lifestyle_supplements.why: Your 25-OH vitamin D is 26.97 ng/mL, below the 30 ng/mL sufficiency threshold.
lifestyle_supplements.caution: Confirm dose and recheck in 8–12 weeks.

citations.title: Evaluation, Treatment, and Prevention of Vitamin D Deficiency

lifestyle_supplements.why: LDL and total cholesterol are substantially above target; lipid-lowering therapy is guideline-indicated for this particle burden.
lifestyle_supplements.caution: Prescription medicine — discuss risks/benefits and monitoring with your doctor.

citations.title: ACC/AHA Guideline on the Management of Blood Cholesterol
citations.claim_supported: LDL-C and ApoB targets for ASCVD risk reduction; Lp(a) one-time testing.

lifestyle_supplements.why: Interest in NAD+ precursors for ageing.

citations.title: NMN supplementation and NAD+ precursors in ageing
citations.claim_supported: NMN may raise NAD+ levels in humans (short-term studies only). No long-term human outcome RCT.
citations.conflict_disclosure: David Sinclair has commercial interests in NMN.

gaps_clinician_missed: ApoB tested only twice despite persistently elevated LDL — the better atherogenic marker is undersampled.
Lp(a) measured but not trended — it is a one-time genetic test; the result should inform lifetime cardiovascular risk.
eGFR reported less often than creatinine; kidney filtration trend is under-tracked given the latest value below 90.
Fasting insulin tested only a handful of times — HOMA-IR cannot be trended, so insulin resistance relies on the TyG surrogate.
